# Handoff Intro Message v1

Use this as a short introduction when sharing the transfer scaffold with a new collaborator.

Use it before you send the heavier transfer template or any project-specific addendum.

## Short version

This is not a finished all-in-one system. It is a scaffold for making collaboration with AI more stable and less random.

The roles support a human-led feedback loop. They help a person structure reading, feedback, and handoff, but they do not self-coordinate independently of that person.

If different role readings pull in different directions, treat them as weighted feedback rather than as votes. A short human steering message can reset what matters most before the next pass.

The main point is not that you learn a role structure immediately. The main point is that you can start using it with low friction.

If you want, you can begin simply with Echo as a sparring partner. Anchor is more of a support layer for keeping direction and reducing drift when needed, not something you have to think about from the start.

When Anchor is used, treat it as a review and drift-control layer that a human invokes, not as a separate decision-maker that takes over the collaboration.

This package mainly carries ways of preserving context and avoiding misreading, not the full project context or any finished identity for you.

If something feels unnecessarily heavy, it is better to scale it down than to make you do more setup.

## Even shorter version

This is a lightweight scaffold for better AI collaboration, not a finished system.

Start simply. Echo can be your sparring partner right away. Anchor is only used when we need to hold direction and reduce drift.

If something creates too much friction, it is the wrong level and should be simplified.
